message:
fix(freeze): correct define_array_el stack behavior

The define_array_el opcode was incorrectly popping 2 stack elements
(both value and index) instead of just 1 (value only). This caused
array spread operations like [...arr, elem] to corrupt the stack by
removing the index prematurely, preventing subsequent increment ops.

QuickJS semantics for define_array_el:
- Stack before: [array, idx, val]
- Stack after: [array, idx] (sp -= 1, only val consumed)

The index must remain on stack for the following OP_inc to increment it.

Symptoms fixed:
- Array spread returned garbage values (e.g., 2.121995792e-314)
- [...path, key].join(".") returned undefined
- tRPC "Duplicate key: undefined" error in createRouterInner

Test results after fix:
- Zod: 10/10 tests pass
- RxJS: 27/27 tests pass
- tRPC pattern: All tests pass
- Array spread: All operations work correctly
